description: SESSION_COOKIE_NAME in src/shared/authCookieNames.ts is hardcoded to __Host-better-auth.session_token, but better-auth uses cookiePrefix 'better-auth' on localhost (configured in src/infrastructure/auth.ts line 171). This means the session cookie name on localhost is 'better-auth.session_token', not '__Host-better-auth.session_token'. Consequence: BetterAuthService.signIn() fails to extract the session token from auth.api.signInEmail() responses (cookie name doesn't match), causing all form-based sign-ins to fail with 'Invalid email or password' even when credentials are correct. Also breaks sign-out (can't read session cookie), requireAuth middleware (can't find session cookie), and dashboard access. The JSON API path works fine because it uses auth.handler() directly. Reproduction: sign up via browser form, then try to sign in — always shows 'Invalid email or password'. Verify via API: POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with same credentials returns 200.
title: bug: SESSION_COOKIE_NAME mismatch breaks all form-based auth on localhost

description: Before binding the US04 rate-limiting acceptance test, confirm that the Durable Object binding (RATE_LIMIT) is available in the acceptance vitest project configuration (vitest.config.ts workers project). If missing, add the binding so rate limiting actually executes during acceptance tests instead of being silently skipped.
title: Verify DO bindings in acceptance test vitest config

description: POST /auth/sign-up with valid CSRF, email, and password returns 200 with 'An error occurred. Please try again.' instead of creating the account. SignUpUseCase catches all exceptions silently (line ~120) and converts them to service_error with no logging. Root cause likely in better-auth/D1 interaction. Makes local dev auth flow completely broken.
title: bug: sign-up always fails with generic error in local dev

description: Address findings from automated code quality, architecture, and security reviews on PR #43 (ralph.sh epic descendants fix). Two actionable findings: duplicate function definitions (critical) and unescaped JSON construction (low).
title: fix/pr43-review-findings

description: Implementation parent for PR #43 review finding tasks.
title: [sp:07-implement] PR #43 review remediation

description:
File: ralph.sh
Lines: 812-860

Severity: Critical

Problem:
Three functions (task_has_children, all_children_closed, auto_close_completed_parents) are defined twice — identical copies at lines 716-760 and again at 812-860. In bash, the last definition wins silently. The duplicate all_children_closed at line 820 checks --status open only, missing children with status in_progress. The original at line 724 correctly filters status != closed. Because bash uses the last definition, this regression is live — parents can be auto-closed while children are still in progress.

Fix:
Remove the duplicate block at lines 812-860. The originals at lines 716-760 are correct and already used by sweep_completed_parents.
title: [quality] Remove duplicate function definitions in ralph.sh

description:
File: ralph.sh
Line: 893

Severity: Low

Problem:
printf does not escape JSON metacharacters when constructing JSON payloads. Special characters in values could break JSON structure or cause unexpected behavior.

Fix:
Use jq --arg or printf with proper JSON encoding to safely construct JSON payloads.
title: [security] Escape JSON metacharacters in ralph.sh printf

close_reason: US04 rate-limiting test bound and passing

May require verifying Durable Object bindings are available in the acceptance vitest project.

This unblocks the US04 rate-limiting acceptance test (turtlebased-ts-852).

description: The acceptance test vitest project (vitest-pool-workers) does not expose DurableObject bindings needed by DurableObjectRateLimiter. This unblocks the US04 rate-limiting acceptance test (turtlebased-ts-852).
title: Configure DurableObject bindings in acceptance vitest project

description: The acceptance vitest project cannot test rate-limiting because DurableObjectRateLimiter requires DO bindings that aren't available in the test environment. Task: (1) verify whether DO bindings are declared in the acceptance test vitest config and wrangler.toml test section, (2) add the DurableObjectRateLimiter binding if missing, (3) confirm a minimal DO-dependent test can instantiate the rate limiter without infrastructure errors. This unblocks the US04 rate-limiting acceptance test binding.
title: Configure DurableObject bindings for acceptance test vitest project

close_reason: Generator regex was the real issue, not DO/isolatedStorage
description: The US04 rate-limiting acceptance test body is correctly written but fails due to vitest-pool-workers infrastructure issues. Three fixes needed: (1) Increase timeout on the rate-limiting it() block to ~30s (6+ sign-in attempts × 2 HTTP roundtrips each with DO storage ops exceeds 5000ms default). (2) Add Durable Object state cleanup in beforeEach of setup.ts — currently only D1 tables are cleared, but DO rate-limit counters leak between tests. (3) Evaluate whether isolatedStorage needs to be disabled for the acceptance project's DO bindings to prevent the 'Failed to push isolated storage stack frame' error that cascades when DOs are aborted mid-operation.
title: Fix US04 rate-limiting acceptance test infrastructure (timeout + DO storage isolation)

description: The acceptance vitest project (vitest-pool-workers) does not expose Durable Object bindings, which blocks any acceptance test that exercises rate limiting via DurableObjectRateLimiter. Task: (1) Add DO binding configuration to the acceptance vitest/wrangler config (e.g. durableObjects in wrangler.toml test section or miniflare options), (2) Verify the RATE_LIMITER binding is accessible in acceptance test env, (3) Run the existing US04 rate-limiting stub to confirm the binding is available. Once this is done, the US04 rate-limiting acceptance test (turtlebased-ts-852) can be retried.
title: Configure Durable Object bindings in acceptance vitest project

description: POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with valid JSON payload returns HTTP 500 with Content-Length: 0 and no response body. The better-auth handler delegation in worker.ts:97-100 has no error handling - if getServiceFactory() or authHandler() throws, the exception propagates uncaught to Hono's default error handler which returns a bare 500. Should return a JSON error body and log the underlying exception.
title: bug: JSON API sign-in returns 500 with empty body

description: All dynamic endpoints (auth sign-in, sign-up, sign-out, dashboard, API auth) return 500 Internal Server Error in local development because required environment variables are missing and .dev.vars is not configured.
title: Fix auth local dev 500s

description: Wrangler requires .dev.vars for local dev secrets (BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL). Without this file, getAuth() throws 'BETTER_AUTH_SECRET must be at least 32 characters', causing all dynamic endpoints to return 500.
title: Add .dev.vars with required secrets for local development

description: .dev.vars contains BETTER_AUTH_SECRET and other secrets but is not in .gitignore. If a developer creates .dev.vars and runs git add, secrets would be committed to the repository.
title: Add .dev.vars to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks

description: No documentation exists showing developers what environment variables are required for local development. A .dev.vars.example file with comments and placeholder values provides a discoverable setup reference.
title: Add .dev.vars.example template for developer onboarding

description: auth-store.js is served from hugo/static/js/ with immutable 1-year cache headers but no fingerprint. If a security fix is needed, browsers serve stale versions indefinitely. Move to hugo/assets/js/ and serve via Hugo Pipes fingerprint pipeline (matching CSS pattern), or add a version query parameter in baseof.html. Filed from security review (turtlebased-ts-bjd.7).

close_reason: Regression already fixed by commits ac1ccfc (global setup.ts) and 85c0eaa (preserve imports). Verified: all 25 bound acceptance tests pass. The sole failure is the unbound US04 rate-limiting stub, which predates this feature.
description: 14 acceptance tests across US03 (register), US04 (sign-in), US05 (sign-out), and US06 (access protected pages) fail with 'getAuthForm is not defined'. This is a regression likely caused by a refactor during the 012-auth-static-integration branch. The helper function needs to be restored or the tests updated to use the current API.
title: Fix getAuthForm regression in US03-06 acceptance tests

description: The generateTests() function in acceptance/generator.ts hardcodes only 2 imports (cloudflare:test, vitest) and discards any additional import lines from existingSource on regeneration. This causes 14 acceptance tests to fail with 'getAuthForm is not defined' because their helper imports get blown away. A failing RED test already exists (uncommitted in generator.spec.ts: 'preserves custom import lines from existing source'). Fix: (1) extract non-standard import lines from existingSource in generateTests(), (2) emit them after the hardcoded imports, (3) make the RED test green, (4) re-run acceptance pipeline to regenerate US03-US06 spec files with proper helper imports.
title: Fix generator to preserve custom import lines from existing source

description: The helpers exported from generated-acceptance-tests/helpers.ts (getAuthForm, submitAuthForm, signInAs, extractSessionCookie) are used by multiple acceptance test files (US03, US04, US05, US14, US15) without being imported. Test files are regenerated by the acceptance pipeline so explicit imports are fragile. Fix: import all helpers in generated-acceptance-tests/setup.ts and assign them to globalThis, then add a TypeScript declaration file so the global references type-check. Also add the helpers as auto-imports in the acceptance pipeline so newly generated stubs include them. Verify all acceptance tests pass after the change.
title: Make acceptance test helpers globally available via setup.ts

description: The getAuthForm helper is defined but not available at acceptance test runtime despite commits ac1ccfc (global setup.ts) and 85c0eaa (preserve imports). Debug the actual failure: (1) verify getAuthForm is exported from the helpers module, (2) check that generated-acceptance-tests/setup.ts actually registers it globally or that generated test files import it, (3) run the acceptance pipeline (just acceptance) and trace the exact ReferenceError, (4) fix the wiring gap. This is an acceptance-pipeline infrastructure fix, not a TDD-driven feature — skip the red-unit-test step and work directly on the acceptance test configuration.
title: Debug acceptance test helper wiring for getAuthForm

description: Remove the 'buttons' menu section from hugo/config/_default/menus.yaml (lines 15-18). Add [x-cloak] { display: none \!important; } rule to hugo/assets/css/styles.css. Skills: /hugo-templates. Acceptance: Hugo builds with zero errors/warnings, x-cloak rule present in compiled CSS, no buttons menu entries remain.
title: US1: Remove buttons menu and add x-cloak CSS rule

description:
File: src/presentation/utils/cookieBuilder.ts:104 + src/infrastructure/BetterAuthService.ts:23
Severity: Medium

Problem:
better-auth library implementation detail (__Host-better-auth.session_token) is hardcoded identically in both the infrastructure and presentation layers. If the cookiePrefix config or library version changes, two files in separate layers must be updated in sync.

Fix:
Move SESSION_COOKIE_NAME to src/shared/ (e.g. src/shared/authCookieNames.ts) so both layers consume the single source of truth without violating the dependency rule.
title: [architecture] SESSION_COOKIE_NAME hardcoded in two layers

description:
File: src/presentation/middleware/requireAuth.spec.ts:196-198
Severity: Medium

Problem:
'as never' type casts bypass Hono's typed context — typos in variable names ('user', 'session', 'csrfToken') would pass TypeScript silently.

Fix:
Define type TestVariables = { user: ...; session: ...; csrfToken: string } and type the test Hono instance as Hono<{ Variables: TestVariables }>.
title: [quality] Type-unsafe 'as never' casts in requireAuth spec

description:
File: generated-acceptance-tests/setup.ts:35-50
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Inlined AUTH_MIGRATIONS SQL is manually duplicated from migrations/0001_better_auth_schema.sql with no sync enforcement. If the migration file changes, acceptance tests silently run against a stale schema.

Fix:
Add a build-time check (e.g., a checksum or just rule) that compares the inlined SQL against the source migration and fails if they diverge.
title: [quality] Inlined AUTH_MIGRATIONS diverges from source migration

description:
File: .husky/pre-commit:5
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Full acceptance test suite added to pre-commit hook — acceptance tests are integration tests that can take minutes, blocking every commit.

Fix:
Move npm run test:acceptance to CI only; keep pre-commit to unit tests (test:coverage) and linting.
title: [quality] Acceptance tests in pre-commit hook block commits

description:
File: acceptance/generator.ts:39
Severity: Low

Problem:
Regex for vitest options object breaks on nested braces (e.g., { retry: 2, onFailure: () => {} }) — no test covers this case.

Fix:
Replace [^}]* with a balanced-brace counter or document this as an explicit unsupported case and add a test asserting the limitation.
title: [quality] Generator regex breaks on nested braces in vitest options

description: Set progress to in_progress
title: State change: progress → in_progress

description:
File: src/worker.ts
Line: 44
Severity: Medium

Problem:
/dashboard/* is registered with withRequireAuth but not withSecurityHeaders — dashboard responses lack CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and other hardening headers applied to /api/*, /app/_/*, and /auth/*.

Fix:
Add app.use('/dashboard/*', withSecurityHeaders) before or alongside the auth middleware line.
title: [security] Dashboard routes missing security headers

description:
File: src/presentation/utils/cookieBuilder.ts
Line: 70-72, 81
Severity: Medium (reported in two separate security reviews)

Problem:
auth_status cookie uses plain name without __Host- prefix — a subdomain can inject or override this cookie, causing Alpine.js to display authenticated UI when the user is not signed in. The server-side session gate is unaffected; this is a UI-deception risk only.

Fix:
Rename to __Host-auth_status (requires removing any Domain attribute — none is set — while retaining Secure; Path=/). Update the indicator cookie contract and Alpine.js cookie check string in hugo/assets/js/auth-store.js. Update specs/012-auth-static-integration/contracts/indicator-cookie.md.
title: [security] Indicator cookie lacks __Host- prefix

description:
File: src/application/ports/AuthService.ts
Line: 134
Severity: Medium

Problem:
hasSession(cookieHeader: string) takes a raw cookie header string, leaking HTTP layer knowledge into the application boundary. AuthPageHandlers holds a direct AuthService reference solely to call hasSession(), bypassing the use-case layer.

Fix:
Remove hasSession() from AuthService and its implementation in BetterAuthService.ts. In AuthPageHandlers.handlePostSignOut, replace this.authService.hasSession(cookieHeader) with hasSessionCookie(cookieHeader) from cookieBuilder.ts. Remove the authService constructor parameter and private field from AuthPageHandlers. Update src/di/serviceFactory.ts to stop passing authService to AuthPageHandlers.
title: [architecture] HTTP transport concern in AuthService port

description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts
Line: 129
Severity: High

Problem:
When the CSRF cookie is absent, timingSafeStringEqual is skipped and 403 returns immediately, creating a measurable timing difference that leaks whether the cookie jar contains the CSRF cookie.

Fix:
Always call timingSafeStringEqual(csrfFormToken, csrfCookieToken ?? '') and check the result, eliminating the fast-path.
title: [security] Timing oracle in CSRF validation

description: Refactor hugo/layouts/_partials/shared/header.html: replace the {{ range }} buttons loop (lines 25-33) with two Alpine.js-guarded sections inside a div x-data wrapper. Unauthenticated: Sign In link + Sign Up button (visible by default). Authenticated: Dashboard button (x-cloak, shown via x-show). Include aria-live=polite, aria-label=Account, link-primary for focus visibility. Use optional chaining ($store.auth?.isAuthenticated) per red team finding. Add min-w class to prevent CLS. Skills: /hugo-templates, /htmx-alpine-templates, /prefactoring. Acceptance: Hugo builds clean, both auth states render correctly, progressive enhancement works (no-JS shows Sign In/Sign Up).
title: US1: Replace header buttons loop with Alpine.js auth-aware navbar

description:
File: src/infrastructure/BetterAuthService.ts
Line: 317
Severity: Low

Problem:
hasSession() uses a substring match (cookieHeader.includes('better-auth.session')) which would false-positive on any cookie whose name contains that string.

Fix:
Reuse extractSessionToken(cookieHeader) !== null for precise detection.
title: [security] hasSession uses imprecise substring match

description:
File: src/presentation/utils/securityHeaders.ts
Line: 27
Severity: Low

Problem:
CSP permits unsafe-inline for style-src, enabling CSS injection if user-controlled data ever reaches a style context.

Fix:
Replace Alpine.js x-show with x-cloak + a CSS class toggle to remove the unsafe-inline requirement; document the trade-off until then.
title: [security] CSP permits unsafe-inline for style-src

description:
File: hugo/layouts/dashboard/list.html
Line: 4
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Hardcoded redirect URL %2Fdashboard%2F in x-init — won't track if the page route changes.

Fix:
Use Hugo's .Permalink to build the redirectTo value server-side and pass via a data-* attribute read by Alpine.
title: [quality] Hardcoded redirect URL in dashboard template

description:
File: scripts/check-migration-sync.ts
Line: 92
Severity: Low

Problem:
Hardcoded filename migrations/0001_better_auth_schema.sql in the error message — will mislead if migration files are added or renamed.

Fix:
Pass the migration filename as a parameter to verifyMigrationSync and include it in the message dynamically.
title: [quality] Hardcoded migration filename in error message

description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts
Line: 126
Severity: High

Problem:
timingSafeStringEqual(csrfFormToken, csrfCookieToken ?? '') passes when both values are empty strings — absent form token + absent cookie both coerce to '', and comparing two zero-length byte arrays returns true. A cross-origin POST with no _csrf field succeeds against users who have no __Host-csrf cookie set. This is a regression from the timing oracle fix (turtlebased-ts-bjd.6.29).

Fix:
Reject null CSRF cookie before comparison: if (csrfCookieToken === null || !timingSafeStringEqual(csrfFormToken, csrfCookieToken))
title: [security] CSRF bypass via empty string coercion in timingSafeStringEqual

description:
File: generated-acceptance-tests/US15-dashboard-auth-guard.spec.ts
Line: 34
Severity: Medium

Problem:
expect(res.status).not.toBe(302) passes on 500 or 404, so it does not actually verify the dashboard is accessible to an authenticated user.

Fix:
Replace with expect(res.status).toBe(200) so the acceptance test verifies a successful response.
title: [quality] Weak acceptance test assertion in US15 dashboard auth guard

description:
File: src/infrastructure/BetterAuthService.ts
Line: 127
Severity: High

Problem:
When indexOf returns positions that produce an empty string, an empty token is silently passed as valid.

Fix:
Add `if (sessionToken === '') return { ok: false, kind: 'service_error' };` after extracting the token.
title: [quality] Missing guard for empty sessionToken in BetterAuthService

description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.spec.ts
Lines: 419, 422, 428, 1120, 1130, 1141
Severity: High

Problem:
res.headers.get('Set-Cookie') returns only the first Set-Cookie header. Sign-in/sign-out paths set three separate headers (session, indicator, CSRF-clear), so assertions on the 2nd and 3rd cookies silently pass vacuously.

Fix:
Replace res.headers.get('Set-Cookie') with res.headers.getSetCookie() (returns an array) and use .some(v => v.includes(...)).
title: [quality] Set-Cookie header assertions use .get() instead of .getSetCookie()

description:
File: src/infrastructure/BetterAuthService.spec.ts
Line: 82
Severity: High

Problem:
signIn spy-argument tests use a mock response with no set-cookie header, so the function under test returns { ok: false, kind: 'service_error' } — assertions verify call arguments on a failing code path.

Fix:
Add a set-cookie: better-auth.session_token=test; Path=/ header to the mock response so the success path is actually exercised.
title: [quality] signIn spec mock missing set-cookie header exercises failure path

description:
File: src/application/use-cases/SignOutUseCase.spec.ts
Lines: 55, 66
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Failure-kind assertions live inside if (!result.ok) guards; if the result is unexpectedly ok: true the expect(result.kind) calls silently never run.

Fix:
Add expect.assertions(N) at the top of each failure test, or assert expect(result.ok).toBe(false) unconditionally before the guard.
title: [quality] Guarded failure assertions in SignOutUseCase spec

description: Write specs/acceptance-specs/US13-client-side-auth-status-check.txt with 3 GWT scenarios from spec.md US2. Skills: /acceptance-tests, /glossary. Acceptance: spec file exists, parseable by acceptance pipeline, covers unauthenticated state, authenticated state, and server-authoritative check.
title: US2: Write GWT acceptance spec for client-side auth status check

description:
File: src/application/use-cases/SignUpUseCase.spec.ts
Lines: 115, 127, 138, 149
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Same guarded-assertion pattern across all four failure cases — expect(result.kind) inside if (!result.ok) guards silently never run if result is unexpectedly ok.

Fix:
Add expect.assertions(N) or assert result.ok === false unconditionally first.
title: [quality] Guarded failure assertions in SignUpUseCase spec

description:
File: src/worker.spec.ts
Lines: 19, 23, 27
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Middleware callback params typed as unknown and cast to () => Promise<void>, hiding the real (c: Context, next: Next) => … signature; type mismatches won't be caught.

Fix:
Type the mock parameters with the actual Hono Context and Next types.
title: [quality] Unsafe unknown casts in worker middleware spec

description:
File: src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts
Line: 327
Severity: Medium

Problem:
extractSessionToken(cookieHeader) ?? '' masks null with an empty string and silently forwards '' as the session token to the use case.

Fix:
Narrow the type explicitly — if the value is null after hasSessionCookie, return a 400/redirect instead of coercing to an empty string.
title: [quality] Null-coercion of sessionToken in AuthPageHandlers

description:
File: acceptance/generator.spec.ts
Line: 46
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Optional chaining result?.description after expect(result).not.toBeNull() silently returns undefined instead of failing if the assertion is wrong.

Fix:
Use result!.description after the null assertion, or restructure with if (result === null) throw.
title: [quality] Optional chaining after not.toBeNull() in generator spec

description:
File: generated-acceptance-tests/US13-client-side-auth-status-check.spec.ts
Line: 41
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Sign-in response status is never asserted before reading Set-Cookie; a failed sign-in would cause the subsequent cookie assertion to pass vacuously.

Fix:
Add expect(signInRes.status).toBe(303) before reading the cookie header.
title: [quality] Missing sign-in status assertion in US13 acceptance test

description:
File: generated-acceptance-tests/US14-authenticated-navbar-swap.spec.ts
Lines: 24, 98
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Missing status assertion for sign-in (line 24); expect(res.status).not.toBe(302) (line 98) passes on any non-redirect including 500.

Fix:
Assert expect(signInRes.status).toBe(303) at line 24; replace line 98 with expect(res.status).toBe(200).
title: [quality] Missing and weak status assertions in US14 acceptance test

description:
File: src/shared/authCookieNames.ts
Line: 7
Severity: Low

Problem:
Public exported constant uses a plain // comment instead of a JSDoc /** */ block, violating the JSDoc requirement for public symbols.

Fix:
Change to /** Session cookie name used by better-auth. */
title: [quality] Missing JSDoc on authCookieNames constant

description:
File: src/presentation/utils/securityHeaders.ts
Line: 6
Severity: Low

Problem:
CSP_DIRECTIVES public constant has no JSDoc.

Fix:
Add /** CSP directive strings joined to form the Content-Security-Policy header value. */
title: [quality] Missing JSDoc on CSP_DIRECTIVES constant

description:
File: generated-acceptance-tests/US15-dashboard-auth-guard.spec.ts
Line: 86
Severity: Low

Problem:
expect(signInRes.headers.get('Location')).toBe('/dashboard/') does not first assert signInRes.status === 303, so a stray Location header from a different redirect would pass unnoticed.

Fix:
Add expect(signInRes.status).toBe(303) before the Location header assertion.
title: [quality] Missing status assertion before Location header in US15 spec

description:
File: scripts/check-migration-sync.ts
Line: 28
Severity: Low

Problem:
parseMigrationSql splits on all semicolons including those inside SQL string literals (e.g. DEFAULT 'foo;bar'); no test covers this edge case.

Fix:
Add a test for SQL strings containing semicolons, or add a comment documenting the limitation.
title: [quality] parseMigrationSql splits on semicolons inside string literals

description: TDD in src/presentation/utils/cookieBuilder.ts: add buildAuthIndicatorCookie() and clearAuthIndicatorCookie() following existing pattern (constant name + attributes + function). Cookie: auth_status=1; Secure; SameSite=Lax; Path=/; Max-Age=2592000 (NOT HttpOnly). Add tests to cookieBuilder.spec.ts: 6 tests for build (value, Secure, SameSite, Path, Max-Age, NOT HttpOnly) and 5 for clear. Add JSDoc. Add code comment cross-referencing buildSessionCookie() for Max-Age alignment. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing, /prefactoring, /glossary. Acceptance: all tests green, 100% coverage, cookie is NOT HttpOnly (intentional for JS readability).
title: US2: TDD indicator cookie functions in cookieBuilder

description:
File: acceptance/generator.spec.ts
Line: 47
Severity: Low

Problem:
matchItLine tests only assert result?.description; the matchLength field is never verified despite being used downstream in extractBoundFunctions for offset calculation.

Fix:
Add expect(result!.matchLength).toBe(...) in at least one positive-case test.
title: [quality] matchItLine test missing matchLength assertion

description:
File: src/presentation/utils/securityHeaders.ts
Line: 39
Severity: Medium

Problem:
No Cache-Control: no-store on /dashboard/* responses. Browsers may cache authenticated page HTML; after sign-out, browser back button or direct navigation can serve stale content from cache without hitting the Worker auth guard.

Fix:
Add headers.set('Cache-Control', 'no-store') inside applySecurityHeaders() (affects all worker-first routes, which is appropriate), or scope it to a new applyAuthenticatedPageHeaders() variant used only on dashboard/app routes.
title: [security] Missing Cache-Control no-store on authenticated routes

description:
File: src/infrastructure/BetterAuthService.ts
Line: 132
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Cookie name not validated before token extraction — indexOf('=') skips the cookie name entirely; if better-auth returns any other Set-Cookie header first, the extracted 'token' is silently wrong.

Fix:
Guard with if (!setCookieHeader.startsWith(SESSION_COOKIE_NAME + '=')) return { ok: false, kind: 'service_error' }; before slicing.
title: [quality] Cookie name not validated before token extraction in BetterAuthService

description:
File: acceptance/generator.ts
Lines: 34, 92, 104, 108, 109
Severity: Low

Problem:
Five scattered c8 ignore next comments suppress coverage on lines[i] ?? '' patterns — workarounds for noUncheckedIndexedAccess typing on in-bounds array accesses.

Fix:
Extract a small typed helper (e.g. function lineAt(lines: string[], i: number): string { return lines[i] ?? ''; }) to consolidate the pattern and remove the suppressions.
title: [quality] Consolidate c8 ignore comments in acceptance generator

description:
File: src/application/use-cases/SignInUseCase.ts, src/application/use-cases/SignUpUseCase.ts
Lines: 89, 98
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Rate limiting silently bypassed when CF-Connecting-IP is absent — each unknown-IP request gets a fresh crypto.randomUUID() key, so every attempt starts a fresh counter and the limit is never reached.

Fix:
Use a shared sentinel key (e.g. ratelimit:sign-in:unknown) so all no-IP requests share one counter, or reject with service_error when IP is 'unknown'.
title: [security] Rate limiting bypassed when CF-Connecting-IP absent

description:
File: scripts/check-migration-sync.ts
Line: 46
Severity: Low

Problem:
extractInlinedQueries regex [^\]+ silently mis-parses SQL strings containing ] characters (e.g. D1 JSON operators). This is the same class of limitation as the documented semicolon issue in parseMigrationSql but is undocumented here.

Fix:
Add a comment documenting this limitation alongside the one in parseMigrationSql.
title: [quality] extractInlinedQueries regex limitation undocumented

description:
File: hugo/assets/js/auth-store.js
Line: 14
Severity: Low

Problem:
Cookie name __Host-auth_status=1 is a hardcoded string literal that duplicates the AUTH_INDICATOR_COOKIE_NAME constant from cookieBuilder.ts. A cookie rename requires manual sync of this file.

Fix:
Add an inline comment referencing AUTH_INDICATOR_COOKIE_NAME in src/presentation/utils/cookieBuilder.ts as the source of truth so a future renamer knows to update this file.
title: [quality] Hardcoded cookie name in auth-store.js duplicates TypeScript constant

description:
File: hugo/layouts/dashboard/list.html:5, src/presentation/utils/securityHeaders.ts
Severity: Low

Problem:
Alpine.js 3.15.8 standard build uses new Function() for directive evaluation, which requires CSP unsafe-eval. The Worker applies script-src 'self' (no unsafe-eval) to /dashboard/* responses, silently breaking all Alpine directive evaluation (x-init redirect, :class visibility bindings). Server-side withRequireAuth is unaffected, but the client-side UX guard is non-functional.

Fix:
Replace hugo/static/js/alpine-3.15.8.min.js with the Alpine CSP build (@alpinejs/csp) which uses expression parsing instead of new Function() — no unsafe-eval needed.
title: [security] Alpine.js standard build requires unsafe-eval breaking CSP

description: Modify src/presentation/handlers/AuthPageHandlers.ts: (1) In handlePostSignIn() success branch, append Set-Cookie for buildAuthIndicatorCookie(). (2) In handlePostSignOut(), clear indicator cookie in ALL response branches (success, no-session early return, service error) per red team finding. Add code comment about 303 redirect cookie atomicity. Update import to include buildAuthIndicatorCookie and clearAuthIndicatorCookie. Update AuthPageHandlers.spec.ts with assertions for indicator cookie in sign-in success and all sign-out branches. Add code comment in handlePostSignUp() documenting no indicator cookie assumption. Skills: /typescript-unit-testing, /error-handling-patterns. Acceptance: tests green, indicator cookie set on sign-in, cleared on all sign-out paths, sign-up unchanged.
title: US2: Set/clear indicator cookie in AuthPageHandlers sign-in and sign-out

description: Create hugo/static/js/auth-store.js (or hugo/assets/js/auth-store.js for Hugo Pipes fingerprinting): Alpine.js store registering on alpine:init event, reading document.cookie for exact match 'auth_status=1'. Add store namespace collision comment. Load in hugo/layouts/baseof.html with two defer script tags (auth-store.js THEN alpine-3.15.8.min.js) before </head>. Add HTML comment about CRITICAL defer requirement (NOT async). Skills: /htmx-alpine-templates, /hugo-templates. description: Epic for fixing auth endpoint bugs discovered during local dev testing: sign-up failures, 500 errors on JSON API, missing 405 responses, CSP header bypass on dashboard redirect, and unvalidated redirectTo in GET handlers.
title: fix/auth-local-dev-500s

description: Implementation task for fixing auth endpoint bugs found during local dev testing.
title: [sp:07-implement] fix auth local dev 500s

description: GET /dashboard (no trailing slash) does not match the wrangler.toml run_worker_first pattern '/dashboard/*', so it falls through to ASSETS binding. The ASSETS binding returns a 307 redirect to /dashboard/ with Hugo's weaker CSP headers (includes unsafe-inline, external analytics domains like googletagmanager.com and usefathom.com) instead of the Worker's strict CSP. The Worker's withSecurityHeaders middleware never runs for this request. This is a security header bypass for any /dashboard request without trailing slash.
title: bug: /dashboard without trailing slash bypasses Worker security headers

description: HEAD requests to API endpoints (e.g. HEAD /api/health) return every response header twice, including the HTTP status line. GET requests to the same endpoint return headers correctly (once each). This appears to be related to the withSecurityHeaders middleware that reconstructs the response with 'new Response(c.res.body, c.res)'. Likely the middleware runs twice for HEAD requests or the response reconstruction duplicates headers. Impact: technically violates HTTP spec and may confuse HTTP clients or proxies that parse response headers.
title: bug: HEAD requests to /api/* return duplicate response headers

description: When a user hits the rate limit on auth form endpoints, AuthPageHandlers.buildRateLimitedResponse returns a plain text 'Too Many Requests' response without the styled page template (no HTML layout, no DaisyUI styling, no consistent branding). This creates a jarring UX where the user suddenly sees raw text instead of a styled error page. The sign-in and sign-up error pages correctly use the styled template with alert components, but the rate limit response does not. Should use a styled error page template consistent with other auth error states.
title: bug: rate limit 429 response is unstyled plain text

description: In AuthPageHandlers.handlePostSignIn (line 222-230), all non-ok, non-rate-limited results are displayed as 'Invalid email or password', including service_error (DB unavailable, unexpected better-auth response, etc.). This is misleading — a user with correct credentials who hits a transient server error sees the same message as someone with wrong credentials. The handler should distinguish service_error from invalid_credentials and show 'An error occurred. Please try again.' for server errors (matching the sign-up handler pattern). Currently: service_error → 'Invalid email or password'. Expected: service_error → 'An error occurred. Please try again.'
title: bug: sign-in handler shows 'Invalid email or password' for server errors

description: The pre-commit hook runs 'vitest run --coverage --project=node' which only covers the acceptance pipeline code (acceptance/*.ts), not the generated GWT acceptance test specs (generated-acceptance-tests/*.spec.ts) which run under the 'acceptance' vitest project. This allowed 23 failing acceptance tests to be committed. The hook needs to also run the acceptance project, or 'just acceptance' needs to gate commits.
title: Pre-commit hook does not run acceptance test specs

description: The CSRF cookie in src/presentation/utils/cookieBuilder.ts uses CSRF_COOKIE_NAME='__Host-csrf' with 'Secure' attribute unconditionally. On http://localhost:8787, the __Host- prefix requires Secure:true but HTTP cannot satisfy this. Chrome treats localhost as a secure context and sends the cookie anyway, but Firefox and Safari may silently drop __Host- cookies on plain HTTP, causing all form submissions to fail with 403 Forbidden. The better-auth session cookie correctly drops the __Host- prefix on localhost (auth.ts line 171), but the CSRF cookie does not follow the same pattern. This creates an inconsistency where auth works on Chrome localhost but may fail on other browsers.
title: bug: CSRF cookie uses __Host- prefix with Secure flag on localhost HTTP

description: AuthPageHandlers.handleGetSignIn passes the raw redirectTo query parameter to the login template without calling validateRedirectTo(). The POST handler correctly validates it, so the actual redirect is safe. But the GET handler renders unvalidated values like 'https://evil.com' or 'javascript:alert(1)' into hidden form fields. While HTML-escaped (no XSS) and POST-validated (no open redirect), this is inconsistent with defense-in-depth - the form visibly contains an external URL which could be used in phishing.
title: bug: GET handler for auth pages does not validate redirectTo parameter

description: Only POST /auth/sign-out is registered in worker.ts. When a GET request arrives, Hono falls through to the static asset handler which returns 404. Per HTTP spec, this should return 405 Method Not Allowed with an Allow: POST header. Same pattern affects PUT/DELETE on /auth/sign-in and /auth/sign-up - all return 404 via static asset fallthrough instead of 405.
title: bug: GET /auth/sign-out returns 404 instead of 405

close_reason: Used atomic file creation with noclobber to prevent TOCTOU race condition
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:185-212
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

The lock file check and creation are not atomic. A TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race condition exists between checking if the lock file exists and creating it.

## Risk

Two concurrent executions could both pass the check before either creates the lock, allowing concurrent runs despite the locking mechanism.

## Recommended Fix

Use `flock` or atomic file creation with `noclobber` option:
```bash
set -o noclobber
echo $$ > "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || { echo "Lock exists"; exit 1; }
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:185:quality_
title: Fix lock file race condition in ralph.sh

close_reason: Added is_ralph_running() that validates both PID existence and process identity
description:
**Code Review Finding**

- **Category**: quality
- **File**: ralph.sh:179-181
- **Severity**: Medium

## Description

`kill -0 "$pid"` only checks if a process exists, not if it is actually a ralph.sh instance. PID reuse could lead to false positives.

## Risk

A stale lock file with a reused PID (now belonging to an unrelated process) would block legitimate execution of ralph.sh.

## Recommended Fix

Store process name or command in lock file and verify both PID and process identity:
```bash
is_ralph_process() {
    local pid="$1"
    kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && \
        [[ $(ps -p "$pid" -o comm= 2>/dev/null) == *ralph* ]]
}
```

---

_Duplicate Key: ralph.sh:179:quality_
title: Improve PID validation in lock file check
